title: mentha sylvestris subsp. nemorosa
intro: Mentha sylvestris subsp. nemorosa, commonly known as woodland pennyroyal, is a subspecies of the mint plant native to Europe. This plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to repel insects, soothe digestive issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of woodland pennyroyal include relief from menstrual cramps, improved respiratory function, and reduced inflammation. The leaves and stems of the plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
